description: TASK-135 (FGAP-148) verified: under --dryRun the filing ops resolve each birth-relation counter-endpoint read-only with the live gate's resolver (dangling/unregistered refused with the live rejection text, unit-pinned), run the prospective-cycle check over on-disk relations plus the accepted birth edges with the new item resolving-by-construction (cycle would-reject pinned incl. self-loop), report would-file on clean previews with live/preview agreement across the pinned cases, and never existence-check the new-item endpoint (exemption refname-scoped to same-substrate endpoints). Implementation moved the append preview op-side (dryRun declared on append-block-item; the CLI item-only replica removed), threading dryRun through the append write path so the preview rides the live path with persistence withheld; backed by exported assertBirthEdgesValidForPreview reusing the live gate machinery through one shared per-edge helper. Orchestrator runtime demo on a scratch substrate: dangling preview/live byte-identical refusals, valid preview would-file with substrate unwritten, orientation guard firing identically. Six adversarial audit rounds (scratchpad wave3/audit-135*.md) iterated to zero: the recurring class was text asserting preview/live outcome equivalence beyond the code (docs, op strings, comments, test comments, CHANGELOG entries), retired across every carrier to the per-check covered-checks register; the final re-audit reported zero findings. Known preview-optimism residuals (new-item kind self-skip; write-pipeline invariant gate live-only; upsert item-arm narrower coverage) are disclosed on the doc surfaces and filed as their own gap. Gates: build/check exit 0, six suites zero-fail on branch and merged main (cb505c31). FGAP-148's filed premise correction (the append arm base behavior was a CLI-side item-only replica, not an orientation-only op preview) recorded at gap closure.
